touches.ai_summary: David Ahmed submitted an inbound demo request via website form. Interested in improving forecast accuracy for their sales team. Mentioned current pain with manual process and exploring alternatives including Fireflies.

Initial qualification call with David Ahmed. Discussed ProtonDigital's current forecasting challenges and their eCommerce vertical focus. David recently joined as Head of RevOps (3 months in role) and is building out processes. Team of 50+ revenue operations members need better pipeline visibility.

questions_asked.question: How does your AI conversation intelligence compare to Fireflies?
questions_asked.answer: Our platform goes beyond transcription to provide deal-level risk scoring and forecasting, while Fireflies focuses primarily on call recording and basic analytics.

questions_asked.question: What's driving the forecast accuracy initiative right now?
questions_asked.answer: Leadership wants to tighten up our quarterly planning. We've missed our forecast 3 out of last 4 quarters and it's creating issues with board expectations.

next_steps: David to map out key stakeholders
Follow-up call to dive deeper into use cases

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up qualification call to clarify scope. David walked through their tech stack (Salesforce, Gong, Outreach) and integration requirements. Discussed rollout to 56 revenue team members across sales and customer success.

questions_asked.question: What's the typical implementation timeline?
questions_asked.answer: Most enterprise customers are fully live within 4-6 weeks including integrations and team training.

touches.ai_summary: Email recap of qualification calls with links to case studies from similar eCommerce companies. Shared ROI calculator and proposed agenda for discovery session with technical and exec stakeholders.

questions_asked.question: Can you share references from other eCommerce companies using this for forecasting?
questions_asked.answer: Sent intro to two reference customers: TechStyle Fashion (reduced forecast error by 32%) and Wayfair competitor (improved pipeline visibility).

next_steps: David to review materials and confirm discovery attendees

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email with additional technical documentation on Salesforce integration and data security. David confirmed Penelope Weber (SVP Revenue) and two Account Managers would join discovery call.
next_steps: Discovery call scheduled for next week

touches.ai_summary: Discovery call with David Ahmed and two Account Managers (Zara White, Riley Choi). Discussed detailed workflow for how they currently forecast deals, pain points with data quality, and gaps in their Gong + Salesforce setup. Zara raised questions about AI accuracy and training data.

questions_asked.question: How do you train the AI models to understand our specific sales process?
questions_asked.answer: We use your historical CRM data and call recordings from the past 12-18 months to build custom risk models tuned to your win/loss patterns.

questions_asked.question: What happens if we're already using Fireflies for transcription?
questions_asked.answer: We can integrate with Fireflies transcripts or replace it entirely - most customers consolidate to reduce tool sprawl and cost.

questions_asked.question: What's your current forecast accuracy baseline?
questions_asked.answer: We're hitting about 62% accuracy on close dates and 58% on amounts. Way too much slippage quarter to quarter.

objections: Need to understand how this doesn't create more work for reps
next_steps: Get David intro to Penelope for exec alignment

touches.ai_summary: Continued discovery focusing on technical requirements and data access. David walked through their compliance requirements (SOC2, GDPR) and data retention policies. Discussed reporting needs for Penelope's leadership reviews.

questions_asked.question: Can we control what data gets analyzed and exclude certain sensitive deals?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, you have full control over deal segmentation and can exclude specific accounts or deal types from AI analysis.

next_steps: Prepare demo environment with ProtonDigital data

touches.ai_summary: Email to David confirming technical eval scheduled with Zara White for next week. Attached demo environment credentials and pre-work guide. Also shared agenda for upcoming exec meeting with Penelope.
next_steps: David to brief Penelope before exec call

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email with answers to Zara's pre-work questions about API rate limits, data refresh frequency, and custom field mapping. Included technical architecture diagram and integration guide.

questions_asked.question: How often does the risk scoring update as new call data comes in?
questions_asked.answer: Risk scores refresh in near real-time (within 15 minutes of call end) and we support webhook notifications for score changes.

next_steps: Technical eval session confirmed for tomorrow

touches.ai_summary: Deep technical evaluation with Zara White. Walked through platform capabilities, data model, API architecture, and integration with their existing stack. Zara tested the demo environment extensively and asked detailed questions about model explainability and edge cases. She compared feature-by-feature against Fireflies.

questions_asked.question: How do you handle deals that don't follow typical patterns, like our strategic partnerships?
questions_asked.answer: The model flags low-confidence predictions and allows manual override. You can also create custom segments with different risk models for strategic deals.

questions_asked.question: What's the false positive rate on your risk alerts?
questions_asked.answer: Across our customer base we see about 12-15% false positive rate, but this improves to 8-10% after the first quarter as the model learns your patterns.

questions_asked.question: Can we export the risk scores and predictions back into Salesforce?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we bi-directionally sync all risk scores, sentiment, and predictions to custom fields in Salesforce with configurable update frequency.

objections: Concerned about change management - reps are already juggling too many tools

touches.ai_summary: Executive alignment meeting with Penelope Weber (SVP Revenue) and David Ahmed. Discussed business impact, ROI projections, and strategic fit with ProtonDigital's growth plans. Penelope focused on board-level reporting and reducing forecast variance. Expressed strong interest in moving forward.

questions_asked.question: What kind of ROI are similar companies seeing in year one?
questions_asked.answer: Most enterprise customers see 20-30% improvement in forecast accuracy within first two quarters, which typically translates to better resource planning and 4-6x ROI.

questions_asked.question: What's your timeline for getting this implemented?
questions_asked.answer: We'd like to be live before Q3 planning kicks off in mid-June. That gives us about 8 weeks.

next_steps: David to work with procurement on budget

touches.ai_summary: Proposal review call with David Ahmed. Walked through pricing for 56 seats on automations_plus_consulting tier ($74,400 ACV). Discussed contract terms, implementation timeline, and success metrics. David asked about discount options and training included.

questions_asked.question: What does the consulting tier include beyond the standard automation features?
questions_asked.answer: You get dedicated CSM, quarterly business reviews, custom playbook development, and ongoing model tuning workshops with our data science team.

questions_asked.question: Is there flexibility on the seat count if we want to start with a smaller pilot?
questions_asked.answer: We can discuss a phased rollout, but our enterprise tier is designed for full-team deployment to get accurate forecasting. Starting smaller would limit the model's effectiveness.

next_steps: David to review internally with Penelope and finance

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up proposal call to address questions from David's internal review. Discussed payment terms (annual vs quarterly), auto-renewal terms, and what happens if they exceed 56 seats. Also covered offboarding from Fireflies and transition plan.

questions_asked.question: If we're currently paying for Fireflies, can that budget be reallocated to offset this cost?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, many customers do exactly that. What's your current Fireflies spend? We can help build the budget reallocation narrative for your CFO.

next_steps: David to get final approval from Penelope

touches.ai_summary: Brief exec check-in with Penelope Weber and David Ahmed. Penelope confirmed budget approved and expressed urgency to get started before Q3. Discussed success criteria and how to measure impact on forecast accuracy.
Email to David with formal proposal document, MSA, and DPA. Included implementation timeline, training schedule, and onboarding checklist. Riley Choi (Account Manager) cc'd as procurement contact.
Follow-up email answering procurement questions from Riley Choi about payment terms, liability caps, and data processing addendum. Shared redline-friendly contract template.

questions_asked.question: Can you accommodate net-60 payment terms?
questions_asked.answer: Our standard terms are net-30, but we can discuss net-45 for annual prepay. Net-60 would require executive approval.

next_steps: Legal teams to connect on contract redlines

touches.ai_summary: Email update from David that legal review identified a few redlines around data retention and indemnification. Riley Choi coordinating with their legal team. Timeline pushed slightly but still targeting close before end of month.

Executive alignment call with Penelope Weber to finalize terms and address legal redlines. Discussed data retention compromise (2 years instead of 3) and agreed on liability cap. Penelope confirmed commitment to close this month and wants team trained by early May.

questions_asked.question: If we sign this week, can you guarantee we're live by May 15th?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we can commit to that timeline if we get contract signed and Salesforce access by end of this week.

questions_asked.question: Is there any flexibility on the annual commit? Our CFO is asking about quarterly payment structure.
questions_asked.answer: We can do quarterly payments with a 5% premium on the annual price, which would make it $78,120 total. But annual prepay at $74,400 is our strongest offer.

touches.ai_summary: Email to David confirming contract updates based on legal feedback. Sent clean version incorporating agreed redlines on data retention and indemnification. Requested signature target by end of week.
next_steps: David and Riley to route for signatures

touches.ai_summary: Pricing finalization call with David Ahmed (originally scheduled earlier, was rescheduled due to David's conflict). Confirmed final terms at $74,400 ACV for 56 seats, annual prepay. Discussed discount request (David asked for 10% off) but held firm on pricing given consulting tier and implementation timeline. David agreed to move forward at list price.

questions_asked.question: Is there any room on price if we commit to a multi-year deal?
questions_asked.answer: We can offer 8% discount on year 2 and 12% on year 3 with a three-year commit, but we need to keep year 1 at list price given the consulting and implementation investment.

objections: Price is at the high end of budget, was hoping for some discount
